title: Monsoon Diseases in India: Prevention, Symptoms & Essential Medicines You Must Keep at Home
excerpt: Every monsoon, diseases like dengue, malaria, typhoid and gastroenteritis spike across India. Learn which medicines to stock, prevention tips, and when to rush to the doctor.
content:
Why Does India See a Health Crisis Every Monsoon?

If you have lived through even one Indian monsoon, you already know the pattern — waterlogging, mosquito breeding, contaminated drinking water, and then the inevitable wave of infections that fills every hospital OPD. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), vector-borne diseases alone account for over 17% of all infectious diseases globally, and India bears a disproportionate burden during the July–September window.

The good news? Most monsoon illnesses are preventable, and even when they strike, early treatment with the right medicines can prevent complications. At Adarsh Medical Store, Garhakota, we see this rush every year — and we want our community to be prepared, not panicked.

Top 5 Monsoon Diseases and Their Warning Signs

1. Dengue Fever

Spread by the Aedes aegypti mosquito, dengue causes high fever (104°F), severe headache behind the eyes, joint and muscle pain, and a skin rash. Platelet counts can drop dangerously. There is no specific antiviral — treatment is supportive: Paracetamol for fever (never Ibuprofen or Aspirin, as they increase bleeding risk), ORS for hydration, and monitoring platelet counts daily.

Read our detailed guide on choosing the right painkiller — Paracetamol vs Ibuprofen before self-medicating.

2. Malaria

Caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted through Anopheles mosquitoes, malaria presents with cyclical high fever with chills, sweating, and fatigue. The National Center for Vector Borne Diseases Control (NCVBDC) recommends Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy (ACT) as the first-line treatment for P. falciparum malaria in India.

3. Typhoid

Contaminated water is the primary culprit. Symptoms include sustained high fever, weakness, stomach pain, and constipation or diarrhoea. The standard treatment involves Cefixime 200mg or Azithromycin. We have covered Cefixime extensively in our complete antibiotic guide.

4. Gastroenteritis & Diarrhoea

Loose motions, vomiting, and dehydration — the monsoon trifecta. ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution) is the single most important treatment. Our detailed ORS preparation guide explains exactly how to mix and administer it for children and adults.

5. Leptospirosis

Walking through floodwater exposes you to Leptospira bacteria. Symptoms mimic flu initially but can progress to kidney and liver failure. Doxycycline 100mg is used both for prevention and treatment.

Your Monsoon Medicine Kit Checklist

MedicinePurposeDosage Guidance
Paracetamol 500mgFever & body pain1 tablet every 6 hours, max 4/day
ORS SachetsDehydration from diarrhoea/vomiting1 sachet in 1 litre water
Domperidone 10mgNausea and vomiting1 tablet before meals
Loperamide 2mgAcute diarrhoea (adults only)As directed by physician
Mosquito repellent creamDengue & malaria preventionApply on exposed skin
Cetrizine/LevocetirizineAllergies, cold, runny nose1 tablet at bedtime

All these medicines are available at genuine wholesale prices from our product catalog. For bulk orders for clinics and hospitals, submit a wholesale inquiry.

Prevention Tips That Actually Work

  1. Eliminate stagnant water around your home — cooler trays, flower pots, old tyres are mosquito nurseries.
  2. Boil or purify drinking water — RO alone is not enough during monsoon when contamination levels spike.
  3. Eat freshly cooked food — avoid street food, cut fruits, and raw salads from outside during peak monsoon.
  4. Use mosquito nets and repellents — especially for children and elderly family members.
  5. Keep a digital thermometer at home — monitoring temperature trends helps doctors diagnose faster.

When Should You See a Doctor Immediately?

Do not delay medical help if you notice: fever lasting more than 3 days, blood in stools or vomit, extreme weakness, reduced urination, or rashes with fever. For any emergency medicine needs in the Garhakota-Sagar region, contact Adarsh Medical Store — we are open Mon-Sat, 10 AM to 8:30 PM.

  • Paracetamol on Tata 1mg
  • ORS Sachets on Netmeds

Stay safe this monsoon. Order your monsoon medicine kit via WhatsApp.

title: Essential First Aid Kit for Every Indian Home: Complete Medicine List with Dosages
excerpt: A well-stocked first aid kit can be the difference between a minor inconvenience and a medical emergency. Here is a pharmacist-approved list of 15 must-have medicines for Indian households.
content:
Why Every Indian Home Needs a Proper First Aid Kit

Think about the last time someone in your family had a sudden fever at midnight, a child scraped their knee, or someone had an unexpected allergic reaction. Did you have the right medicines at hand, or did you scramble to find an open pharmacy? A well-organised first aid kit is not a luxury — it is a basic health necessity, especially in smaller towns like Garhakota where the nearest hospital may be 30+ minutes away.

As a pharmacist with 25+ years of experience, I have put together this practical, no-nonsense guide based on what we actually see patients needing most often at Adarsh Medical Store.

The 15 Must-Have Medicines

Category 1: Pain & Fever

MedicineUseAdult DoseNotes
Paracetamol 500mgFever, headache, body ache1-2 tablets every 6 hrsMax 4g/day. Safest painkiller
Ibuprofen 400mgInflammation, toothache1 tablet every 8 hrsTake after food. Avoid if acidity

For a deeper comparison of painkillers, read our Paracetamol vs Ibuprofen vs Diclofenac guide.

Category 2: Stomach & Digestion

MedicineUseAdult Dose
Pantoprazole 40mgAcidity, heartburn1 tablet before breakfast
ORS SachetsDehydration from diarrhoeaMix in 1L water, sip through day
Ondansetron 4mgSevere vomiting1 tablet as needed

Learn the differences between acidity medicines in our Omeprazole vs Pantoprazole comparison.

Category 3: Allergies & Cold

MedicineUseAdult Dose
Cetirizine 10mgAllergies, runny nose, itching1 tablet at night
Nasivion Nasal DropsBlocked nose2-3 drops each nostril

Category 4: Wounds & Skin

ItemUse
Povidone-Iodine (Betadine)Wound cleaning and disinfection
Soframycin CreamMinor cuts, burns, skin infections
Band-aids (various sizes)Covering small wounds
Cotton, Gauze, Crepe bandageDressing wounds, sprains

We stock all surgical supplies and dressing materials from our partner brands including Medigrip.

Category 5: Emergency Essentials

ItemPurpose
Digital ThermometerAccurate fever monitoring
BP Monitor (digital)For hypertension patients
Glucometer + stripsDiabetes monitoring

For managing diabetes medications at home, our Metformin & Glimepiride guide is a must-read, and for BP patients, check our blood pressure medicines guide.

Storage Tips for Indian Conditions

  1. Store medicines in a cool, dry place — NOT the bathroom (humidity damages tablets).
  2. Keep away from direct sunlight and away from children's reach.
  3. Check expiry dates every 3 months and replace expired medicines.
  4. Store liquid medicines like syrups in the refrigerator once opened.
  5. Keep a list of family members' allergies taped inside the kit.

Where to Buy a Complete Kit

You can buy all these items individually or as a bundled first aid kit from Adarsh Medical Store's product catalog. We source from Dr. Reddy's, Mankind Pharma, and other top manufacturers — all genuine, authorized stock.

For hospitals and clinics needing bulk supplies, submit an inquiry for wholesale pricing.

title: Antibiotic Resistance in India: Why Self-Medication is Dangerous & How to Use Antibiotics Safely
excerpt: India is the world's largest consumer of antibiotics, and resistance is now a public health emergency. Learn why popping antibiotics without a prescription is risky and what you should do instead.
content:
India's Antibiotic Crisis — The Numbers Are Alarming

Here is a fact that should worry every Indian: our country consumes more antibiotics than any other nation on Earth. A study published in the Lancet found that antibiotic consumption in India increased by 65% between 2000 and 2015. The result? Bacteria are evolving faster than we can develop new drugs, and common infections that were once easily treatable are becoming life-threatening.

At Adarsh Medical Store, Garhakota, we see this problem daily. Patients walk in asking for "that antibiotic tablet my neighbour took for cold" — without a prescription, without a diagnosis, and often for viral infections where antibiotics are completely useless.

What Exactly is Antibiotic Resistance?

When you take an antibiotic, it kills the sensitive bacteria causing the infection. But some bacteria naturally have mutations that let them survive. When you stop the course early or take the wrong antibiotic, these resistant bacteria survive, multiply, and spread. The next time you get infected, that same antibiotic simply will not work.

The WHO has declared antimicrobial resistance one of the top 10 global public health threats facing humanity.

Common Mistakes Indians Make with Antibiotics

MistakeWhy It's DangerousWhat To Do Instead
Taking antibiotics for cold/fluColds are viral — antibiotics only kill bacteriaRest, fluids, Paracetamol for fever
Stopping course when feeling betterSurviving bacteria become resistantAlways complete the full prescribed course
Sharing antibiotics with familyWrong drug, wrong dose for different infectionsEach person needs their own diagnosis
Using leftover antibioticsMay be expired or wrong for current infectionDispose of unused medicines properly
Demanding antibiotics from chemistSchedule H drugs require prescription by lawSee a doctor first, then buy medicines

How to Use Antibiotics Responsibly

  1. Only take antibiotics prescribed by a qualified doctor after proper diagnosis. No exceptions.
  2. Complete the full course — even if you feel better by day 3 of a 5-day course, finish all 5 days.
  3. Take at the right time — some antibiotics work best on an empty stomach, others with food. Follow the label.
  4. Never share or save — your Cefixime is not your spouse's Cefixime. Different infections need different antibiotics at different doses.
  5. Report side effects — diarrhoea, rash, or itching during an antibiotic course should be reported to your doctor immediately.

Our complete guide to Cefixime covers proper usage of one of India's most prescribed antibiotics.

The Role of Pharmacists in Fighting Resistance

As responsible pharmaceutical distributors, Adarsh Medical Store follows strict dispensing practices. We stock antibiotics from verified manufacturers like Dr. Reddy's, Macleods, and Mankind — companies that invest in quality control and drug safety. We encourage all our customers to bring prescriptions for Schedule H and H1 drugs.

The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) has been running awareness campaigns about antibiotic resistance. As a community, we must support these efforts.

When DO You Actually Need Antibiotics?

  • Bacterial throat infections (confirmed by rapid strep test or culture)
  • Urinary tract infections (confirmed by urine culture)
  • Skin infections with pus (bacterial, not fungal — for fungal infections, read our antifungal guide)
  • Pneumonia (diagnosed by doctor after chest X-ray)
  • Typhoid (confirmed by Widal or blood culture test)

For genuine, authorized medicines with proper expiry dates, visit our complete product catalog or contact us directly.

  • Antibiotics on Tata 1mg
  • Azithromycin on Netmeds

title: Calcium & Iron Deficiency in Indian Women: Symptoms, Best Supplements, and Diet Tips
excerpt: Over 50% of Indian women suffer from anaemia and calcium deficiency. A practical guide to understanding symptoms, choosing the right supplements, and foods that actually help absorption.
content:
A Silent Health Crisis Among Indian Women

Walk into any government hospital OPD in Madhya Pradesh, and you will find the same story repeating — young women complaining of constant tiredness, brittle nails, hair fall, and joint pain. The diagnosis is almost always the same: iron deficiency anaemia, calcium deficiency, or both.

According to the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, the National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5) found that 57% of Indian women aged 15-49 are anaemic. Calcium deficiency is equally widespread, with studies showing over 80% of Indian women have inadequate calcium intake.

At Adarsh Medical Store, we stock a comprehensive range of iron and calcium supplements from trusted manufacturers. But before you buy any supplement, let us understand what your body actually needs.

Iron Deficiency: More Than Just Tiredness

Symptoms to Watch For

  • Persistent fatigue even after adequate sleep
  • Pale skin, especially on the inner eyelids and nail beds
  • Breathlessness during routine activities like climbing stairs
  • Unusual cravings for ice, clay, or starch (a condition called Pica)
  • Frequent infections due to weakened immunity
  • Heavy menstrual periods (which also cause further iron loss — a vicious cycle)

Best Iron Supplements Available

SupplementIron ContentAdditional NutrientsBest For
Ferrous Sulphate 200mg60mg elemental ironNoneMild deficiency, budget option
Ferrous Ascorbate + Folic Acid100mgFolic Acid 1.5mgPregnant women, severe anaemia
Iron Sucrose IVVariableNoneSevere cases, hospital use only

Important tip: Always take iron supplements with Vitamin C (lemon water or orange juice) for better absorption. Never take iron with milk, tea, or coffee — they block absorption by up to 60%.

Calcium Deficiency: Your Bones Are Quietly Weakening

Warning Signs

  • Frequent muscle cramps, especially at night
  • Tingling or numbness in fingers
  • Weak, brittle nails
  • Tooth decay despite good oral hygiene
  • Back pain and joint stiffness

Best Calcium Supplements

SupplementCalcium ContentVitamin D3 Added?Best For
Calcium Carbonate 500mg200mg elementalOften yesGeneral supplementation
Calcium Citrate 1000mg210mg elementalYes (usually 250IU)Those with low stomach acid
Shelcal 500500mg + D3 250IUYesMost popular in India

Calcium works hand-in-hand with Vitamin D3 — without adequate Vitamin D, your body simply cannot absorb calcium properly. Read our complete Vitamin D guide to understand why 70% of Indians are deficient.

Iron + Calcium: Can You Take Both Together?

No! This is one of the most common mistakes. Iron and calcium compete for absorption in your gut. If you take both together, neither gets absorbed properly. The correct approach:

  • Take iron in the morning with Vitamin C (empty stomach or light breakfast)
  • Take calcium at night with dinner (calcium also helps with sleep)
  • Maintain at least a 2-hour gap between the two

Diet Tips for Indian Women

Iron-Rich Foods

Jaggery (gur), spinach (palak), beetroot, pomegranate, dates, rajma, chana, ragi, and organ meats like liver. Cooking in iron kadhai (cast iron) genuinely increases iron content of food.

Calcium-Rich Foods

Milk, curd, paneer, ragi, sesame seeds (til), dried figs (anjeer), almonds, and green leafy vegetables like moringa (drumstick leaves).

When to See a Doctor

If your haemoglobin drops below 8 g/dL, oral supplements alone may not be enough — you may need iron injections. For calcium, a DEXA scan can assess bone density. Women above 40 should get tested annually.

For all your supplement needs, browse our product catalog or visit Adarsh Medical Store in person. We stock supplements from Mankind, Macleods, Dr. Reddy's and more.

For wholesale orders for clinics and hospitals, submit an inquiry here.

  • Iron + Folic Acid on Tata 1mg
  • Shelcal 500 on PharmEasy

title: Complete Guide to Acidity Medicines: Omeprazole vs Pantoprazole — Which is Better?
excerpt: A detailed comparison of the two most popular acidity medicines in India. Learn about their uses, dosage, side effects, and which one your doctor recommends.
content:
Understanding Acidity and Acid Reflux

Acidity, medically known as Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD), is one of the most prevalent health issues affecting millions of Indians. It occurs when the stomach produces excess hydrochloric acid, which then flows back into the esophagus, causing a burning sensation in the chest and throat.

The global pharmaceutical industry has developed several classes of drugs to combat acidity, but the most effective and widely prescribed are Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs). Two of the most popular PPIs available at Adarsh Medical Store, Garhakota, are Omeprazole (sold as Omez) and Pantoprazole (sold as Zovanta by Dr. Reddy's). You can view our complete catalog of Dr. Reddy's products here.

How Do Proton Pump Inhibitors Work?

PPIs work by irreversibly blocking the proton pump in the stomach. Unlike antacids that merely neutralize existing acid, PPIs prevent acid from being produced in the first place, providing longer-lasting and more effective relief.

Omeprazole (Omez) vs Pantoprazole (Zovanta)

ParameterOmeprazolePantoprazole
Speed of ReliefFaster — 30 minModerate — 1-2 hours
Acid Suppression Duration12-17 hours15-24 hours
Drug InteractionsMore — avoid with ClopidogrelFewer — safer choice
Best ForAcute, occasional acidityChronic GERD, long-term use
Price Range₹2-5 per tablet₹5-12 per tablet

Where to Buy Genuine Acidity Medicines

For residents of Garhakota, Sagar, and surrounding areas, Adarsh Medical Store provides genuine, authorized medicines from leading pharmaceutical companies.

  • Buy Pantoprazole on Tata 1mg
  • Buy Omeprazole on Netmeds

title: Cefixime Antibiotic: Complete Guide to Uses, Dosage, and Side Effects
excerpt: Everything you need to know about Cefixime 200mg tablets — one of the most prescribed antibiotics in India for throat, ear, and urinary tract infections.
content:
What is Cefixime?

Cefixime is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic that fights bacteria in the body. It is one of the most widely prescribed oral antibiotics, known for its broad-spectrum activity against both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

At Adarsh Medical Store in Garhakota, Cefixime is available as Cefiwok CV (Cefixime 200mg + Clavulanic Acid 125mg) and Cefiwok-O, both manufactured by Dr. Reddy's Laboratories.

Dosage Guidelines

Patient GroupDosageFrequencyDuration
Adults (above 18 years)200mg tabletTwice daily (every 12 hours)5-7 days
Children (6-12 years)100mg tablet or suspensionTwice daily5 days
Typhoid Fever200mg tabletTwice daily10-14 days

Common Side Effects

Like all medications, Cefixime can cause side effects. The most common is Diarrhea, followed by nausea and abdominal pain. If you experience severe allergic reactions, discontinue use and consult a doctor immediately.

title: Diabetes Management: Metformin and Glimepiride — A Complete Patient Guide
excerpt: Learn how to manage Type 2 Diabetes effectively with the right medications. A comprehensive guide covering Metformin, Glimepiride, diet tips, and monitoring.
content:
Understanding Type 2 Diabetes

Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is a chronic metabolic disorder affecting the body's ability to use insulin. With over 101 million cases, managing diabetes through diet and medication is crucial. The most commonly prescribed oral anti-diabetic drugs are Metformin and Glimepiride.

Metformin vs Glimepiride

Metformin (Glycomet, Obimet) is the first-line treatment. It decreases liver glucose production and increases insulin sensitivity. Glimepiride (Amaryl) is added when Metformin alone isn't enough, working by stimulating the pancreas to release more insulin.

MedicationStarting DoseMaximum DoseWhen to Take
MedicationStarting DoseMaximum DoseWhen to Take
Metformin500mg once daily2000mg/day (divided)With or after meals
Glimepiride1mg once daily8mg/dayWith breakfast
Combination (Met+Glim)500mg/1mg1000mg/4mgWith breakfast

Blood Sugar Monitoring & Diet

Regular monitoring is essential. Choose complex carbohydrates, include high-fiber vegetables, and avoid packaged juices. For patients in the Sagar region, Adarsh Medical Store offers complete diabetes care equipment including glucometers and test strips.

title: Antifungal Creams Guide: Clotrimazole, Ketoconazole, and Managing Skin Infections
excerpt: A comprehensive guide to treating common fungal skin infections including ringworm, athlete's foot, and jock itch with the right antifungal medications.
content:
Fungal Skin Infections in Tropical Climates

Fungal skin infections (dermatophytosis) account for 20-25% of dermatology consultations in India. The humid climate promotes infections like Tinea Corporis (ringworm) and Tinea Pedis (athlete's foot).

Top Antifungal Medicines

Here are the best treatments available at our retail store:

  • Candigurd B Cream (Clotrimazole + Beclometasone): Combats the fungus while reducing itching and redness.
  • Ketoluxe ZPTO Shampoo: Best for severe dandruff and scalp fungal issues.
  • Eclospan Cream: A triple-action formula for mixed bacterial/fungal infections.

Proper Application Technique

  1. Wash with mild soap and pat completely dry.
  2. Apply a pea-sized amount, extending beyond the visible edge.
  3. Apply twice daily and continue for 1-2 weeks after symptoms clear to prevent recurrence.
  • Clotrimazole Cream on Tata 1mg

Contact Adarsh Medical Store Garhakota for wholesale supply of ointments.

title: ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution): The Life-Saving Treatment for Dehydration
excerpt: A detailed guide to understanding ORS — how it works, when to use it, how to prepare it correctly, and why it is considered one of the greatest medical discoveries.
content:
What is Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS)?

Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) is a precisely formulated mixture of glucose and electrolytes designed to treat dehydration rapidly. At Adarsh Medical Store, we stock Rebalanz ORS by Dr. Reddy's — a WHO-approved formula available in multiple flavors.

WHO-Recommended ORS Formula

The current WHO-UNICEF recommended formula produces a solution with an osmolarity of 245 mOsm/L, proven to be highly effective. It is critical for treating diarrheal diseases, heat exhaustion, and high fevers in children.

Dosage Guidelines

Age GroupAmount After Each Loose StoolAmount Per Day
Under 2 years50-100 ml500 ml
2-10 years100-200 ml1000 ml
Above 10 years / Adults200-400 mlAs needed

How to Prepare

Mix one sachet in exactly 1 litre of clean, boiled, and cooled drinking water. Do not add extra sugar or salt. Use within 24 hours.

  • Bulk ORS Sachets on Netmeds

title: Blood Pressure Medications Explained: Amlodipine, Telmisartan, and How to Control Hypertension
excerpt: A complete guide to understanding high blood pressure medications, how they work, their side effects, and essential lifestyle tips for keeping your BP under control.
content:
Understanding Hypertension

High blood pressure is a silent killer affecting over 20% of the Indian population. Normal pressure is below 120/80 mmHg. Stage 1 hypertension begins at 130/80 mmHg. Regular monitoring using home devices is critical.

Major Classes of Medications

1. Amlodipine (Calcium Channel Blocker): Relaxes blood vessels. Works for 24 hours with a single dose.
2. Telmisartan (ARB): Blocks the hormone that constricts blood vessels. Protects the kidneys and heart.
3. Metoprolol (Beta-Blocker): Slows the heart rate to reduce pumping pressure.

Lifestyle Management

Medication MUST be paired with lifestyle changes: restrict daily sodium to under 5g, exercise for 150 minutes weekly, and practice yoga for stress reduction. You can explore our cardiovascular partner brands.

  • Amlodipine on Tata 1mg
  • BP Medicines on PharmEasy

title: Pain Relief Medicines: Paracetamol vs Ibuprofen vs Diclofenac
excerpt: A practical guide to choosing the right painkiller for headaches, body pain, fever, and inflammation. Understand doses, risks, and when to see a doctor.
content:
Comparing Pain Relievers

Not all painkillers are the same. Choosing the wrong medication can lead to stomach ulcers, liver damage, or kidney issues. Here is a breakdown of the three most common options supplied by Adarsh Medical Stores.

FeatureParacetamolIbuprofenDiclofenac
Pain ReliefMild-ModerateModerateStrong
Anti-inflammatoryNoYesYes (Strong)
Fever ReductionYesYesYes
Stomach SafetySafeModerate riskHigher risk

Safety Rules

  1. Never take two different NSAIDs (like Ibuprofen and Diclofenac) together.
  2. If pain persists beyond 3-5 days, visit a doctor safely.
  3. Paracetamol overdose is extremely dangerous to the liver. Do not exceed 4000mg/day.
  • Paracetamol on Tata 1mg

title: Vitamin D Deficiency: Symptoms, Treatment, and Best Supplements in India
excerpt: Over 70% of Indians are Vitamin D deficient. Learn the symptoms, causes, best supplements, and how to get tested and treated for this widespread health issue.
content:
The Vitamin D Epidemic

Despite abundant sunshine, over 70% of Indians suffer from Vitamin D deficiency. Symptoms include persistent fatigue, frequent bone pain, slow wound healing, and hair loss. Vitamin D is essential not just for bones, but for a robust immune system.

Treatment Protocols

The standard treatment in India is a loading dose of 60,000 IU Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3) once weekly for 8 weeks. Maintenance doses follow thereafter. We provide wholesale Vitamin D supplements from verified partners like Mankind and Macleods.

Level (ng/mL)StatusAction Required
Below 20DeficientImmediate 60,000 IU weekly protocol
20-29InsufficientDaily Supplementation
30-100SufficientMaintenance (Sunlight)
